methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Durin is a pre-seed stage infrastructure and logistics company founded by Ted Feldmann that has raised $3.4M to date. The relevant comp universe in infrastructure and logistics is sparse, consisting of two identified private peers (AiDash and Watoga Technologies) and no public comparables. A same-stage and adjacent-stage peer median could not be reliably constructed due to insufficient datapoints meeting narrow filtering criteria.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using a $12.0B total raised proxy as the base value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $30.0B. This method represents the single quantitative anchor available given the thinness of the peer set.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $25.5B (low) to $34.5B (high), with a median estimate of $30.0B. The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single methodological pillar. The infrastructure and logistics sector lacks sufficient public and private comparables at durin's stage to triangulate via peer multiples or market precedent. Validation of these stage-progression assumptions against broader sector mobility patterns would require a denser comparable universe than currently available.
